After Saul’s death, many went to David at Ziklag, but he trusted in God who promised him the kingdom, to give it in his own time and manner. Yet assurance of hope in God's promise will encourage good efforts. If I am chosen for the crown of life, it does not follow that I will do nothing; but rather, I will do all that God directs me. This is how David used his election, and so will all whom God has chosen. In all our journeys and moves, it is comforting to see God going before us; and we may do so if by faith and prayer we set Him before us. God, according to the promise, directed David's path. David rose gradually: thus the kingdom of the Messiah, the Son of David, is established by degrees; he is Lord of all, but we do not yet see all things put under him.

The nation in general refused David. By this, the Lord trained up his servant for future honor and usefulness; and his true godliness was shown in his behavior while passing through various difficulties. David was a type of Christ, whom Israel would not submit to, though anointed by the Father to be a Prince and a Savior to them. Abner meant, Let the young men fight before us, when he said, Let them play before us: fools thus mock at sin. But he is unworthy the name of a man who can trifle with human blood.
Death often comes by ways we least suspect. We are often betrayed by the accomplishments we are proud of! Asahel’s swiftness, which he relied on so much, did him no service but hastened his end.

Abner appeals to Joab concerning the miserable consequences of a civil war. Those who make light of such unnatural contests will find that they are bitterness to all concerned. How easy it is for men to use reason when it makes for them, who would not use it if it made against them! See how the outcome of things alters men’s minds! The same thing which looked pleasant in the morning looked dismal at night. Those who are most eager to enter into contention will repent before they have finished with it, and had better leave it off before it is meddled with, as Solomon advises. This is true of every sin; oh that men would consider it in time, that it will be bitterness in the end! Asahel’s funeral is here mentioned. Distinctions are made between the dust of some and that of others; but in the resurrection no difference will be made, except between the godly and ungodly, which will remain forever.
